page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 4 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities for standard compute tasks, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance falling into the middle 50% tier. Large-block memory bandwidth reads and writes also register average performance at 71.58 GB/sec and 34.94 GB/sec respectively. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in specialized workloads, such as single-threaded gzip compression, Geekbench multi-core scoring, and Passmark database operations. Cryptographic acceleration is supported via hardware AES, but advanced vector extensions are absent.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 Graviton architecture combined with high memory density and integrated NVMe storage offers a resource-dense profile for memory-bound applications without requiring external block storage. This makes the r6gd.xlarge highly suitable for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, and medium-scale database operations that benefit from local high-speed scratch space and a high RAM-to-CPU ratio.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated ARM64 cores powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes a bundled 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance in stress-ng CPU tests and large-block memory bandwidth, though it exhibits weaker results in cryptographic and single-threaded decompression tasks. The combination of high memory density, local NVMe storage, and energy-efficient ARM architecture makes this instance a cost-effective choice for memory-intensive workloads, including caching layers, in-memory databases, and data processing applications requiring fast local scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 4 Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance with 4 Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 237 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 cores at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core
Includes 237 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
Delivers average CPU and large-block memory bandwidth performance
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data processing
